title: [API] health: GET /api/health returns 200 with ok status
preconditions: Application is running with seeded employees.
Backend server is started and MongoDB is connected.

steps.action: Send GET /api/health with header Accept: application/json
steps.expectedResult: HTTP status code is 200

steps.action: Inspect the response Content-Type header
steps.expectedResult: Content-Type header contains 'application/json'

steps.action: Parse the response JSON body and inspect the 'status' field
steps.expectedResult: Response JSON contains field 'status' with value 'ok'

steps.action: Inspect the response JSON body for a 'mongo' or 'db' connectivity field (if present)
steps.expectedResult: If a database connectivity field is present, its value indicates a connected/healthy state (e.g. 'connected', true, or 'ok')

steps.action: Re-send GET /api/health a second time immediately after step 1
steps.expectedResult: HTTP status code is still 200 and 'status' field is still 'ok', confirming idempotency of the health endpoint

expectedOutcome: GET /api/health consistently returns HTTP 200 with a JSON body containing status: 'ok', confirming both the API server and MongoDB connection are healthy.

title: [API] health: GET /api/health does not accept unsupported HTTP methods
preconditions: Application is running with seeded employees.
Backend server is started and MongoDB is connected.

steps.action: Send POST /api/health with header Content-Type: application/json and an empty JSON body {}
steps.expectedResult: HTTP status code is 404 or 405 (Method Not Allowed), confirming POST is not a supported method on this endpoint

steps.action: Send DELETE /api/health with no request body
steps.expectedResult: HTTP status code is 404 or 405, confirming DELETE is not a supported method on this endpoint

steps.action: Send PUT /api/health with header Content-Type: application/json and body {}
steps.expectedResult: HTTP status code is 404 or 405, confirming PUT is not a supported method on this endpoint

steps.action: Send GET /api/health again to confirm the endpoint is still operational after the unsupported method attempts
steps.expectedResult: HTTP status code is 200 and response JSON contains 'status': 'ok', confirming the health endpoint was not affected by the invalid method calls

expectedOutcome: Unsupported HTTP methods (POST, DELETE, PUT) against GET /api/health are rejected with 404 or 405, while a subsequent valid GET still returns 200 with status 'ok'.

title: [UI] employee-list: Page loads and renders employee table with data
preconditions: Application is running with seeded employees.
At least one employee record exists in the database (seeded data).
No authentication required — app is publicly accessible.

steps.action: Open a browser and navigate to /
steps.expectedResult: The Employees page loads without any error banners or crash screens; the page title or heading containing 'Employee' is visible

steps.action: Wait for the employee table to finish loading (spinner or skeleton disappears)
steps.expectedResult: A table element is visible on the page with at least one data row rendered; no 'loading' indicator remains

steps.action: Inspect the table header row
steps.expectedResult: Column headers are visible and include labels such as 'Name', 'Email', 'Department', and 'Position' (or equivalent column headings defined in EmployeeTable.tsx)

steps.action: Count the number of data rows rendered in the table body
steps.expectedResult: At least one <tr> element is present inside <tbody>; the row count matches the number of employees returned by GET /api/employees for the current page

steps.action: Verify the first data row contains non-empty text in each visible column cell
steps.expectedResult: Each cell in the first row displays a non-blank value (name, email, department, position); no cell shows 'undefined', 'null', or is visually empty

steps.action: Scroll to the bottom of the page and check for a pagination control or row-count summary
steps.expectedResult: A pagination component or a record-count label (e.g. 'Showing 1–10 of N employees') is visible, confirming the table is data-bound and paginated correctly

expectedOutcome: The employee list page renders successfully on first load, displaying a populated table with correct column headers, at least one employee row with valid field values, and a pagination or count indicator — confirming end-to-end data flow from GET /api/employees to the UI.

title: [UI] employee-list: Table displays empty-state message when no employees match a filter
preconditions: Application is running with seeded employees.
The employee list page has a search or filter input available.
No authentication required — app is publicly accessible.

steps.action: Open a browser and navigate to /
steps.expectedResult: The Employees page loads and the employee table is visible with at least one row

steps.action: Wait for the employee table to finish loading (spinner or skeleton disappears)
steps.expectedResult: Table body contains at least one data row; no loading indicator is present

steps.action: Locate the search or filter input field on the page
steps.expectedResult: A text input for searching or filtering employees is visible and interactive

steps.action: Click the search/filter input and type a string that is guaranteed to match no employee — use a random UUID-like string (e.g. 'zzz-no-match-99999')
steps.expectedResult: The input field accepts the typed text and displays it
steps.testData: value: 'zzz-no-match-99999' (a string with no realistic match in seeded data)

steps.action: Wait for the table to re-render or for a network request to complete after typing
steps.expectedResult: The table body no longer shows any employee data rows; the previous rows have been removed from the DOM

steps.action: Inspect the area where table rows were previously displayed
steps.expectedResult: An empty-state message is visible (e.g. 'No employees found', 'No results', or equivalent text defined in EmployeeTable.tsx); no error banner or crash screen is shown
steps.testData: selector: [data-testid=empty-state] or text matching /no employees/i or /no results/i

steps.action: Clear the search input field completely (select all and delete, or click a clear button if present)
steps.expectedResult: The input field is empty

steps.action: Wait for the table to re-render after clearing the search
steps.expectedResult: The employee table repopulates with the original seeded employee rows; the empty-state message is no longer visible

expectedOutcome: When a search term matching no employees is entered, the table gracefully displays an empty-state message instead of crashing or showing stale data. Clearing the search restores the full employee list, confirming the filter is reactive and non-destructive.

title: [API] employee-list: GET /api/employees returns paginated first page with correct response shape
preconditions: Application is running with seeded employees.
At least one employee record exists in the database.

steps.action: Send GET /api/employees with no query parameters
steps.expectedResult: Response status is 200

steps.action: Assert response Content-Type header contains 'application/json'
steps.expectedResult: Content-Type header value includes 'application/json'

steps.action: Assert response body is a JSON object (not a raw array)
steps.expectedResult: Response body is a JSON object with top-level keys

steps.action: Assert response body contains a top-level 'data' field that is an array
steps.expectedResult: 'data' field exists and is an array with length >= 1

steps.action: Assert response body contains a top-level 'total' field that is a number
steps.expectedResult: 'total' field exists and is a number >= 1

steps.action: Assert response body contains a top-level 'page' field
steps.expectedResult: 'page' field exists and equals 1 (or the default first page value)

steps.action: Assert response body contains a top-level 'limit' (or 'pageSize') field that is a positive integer
steps.expectedResult: 'limit' or 'pageSize' field exists and is a positive integer (e.g. 10 or 20)

steps.action: Inspect the first element of the 'data' array and assert it contains the required employee fields: '_id', 'firstName', 'lastName', 'email', 'designation', 'department', 'employmentType', 'employmentStatus', 'startDate'
steps.expectedResult: First element of 'data' array contains all required fields with non-null, non-empty values

steps.action: Assert the length of the 'data' array does not exceed the value of the 'limit' (or 'pageSize') field
steps.expectedResult: data.length <= limit, confirming pagination is applied and the response is not returning all records unbounded

steps.action: Send GET /api/employees?page=1&limit=5
steps.expectedResult: Response status is 200 and 'data' array length is <= 5

expectedOutcome: GET /api/employees returns HTTP 200 with a JSON object containing a paginated 'data' array, a 'total' count, and pagination metadata. Each employee record in 'data' contains all required fields. The number of records returned does not exceed the page limit.

steps.expectedResult: Response status is 400 or 422; response body contains an error message indicating 'limit' must be >= 1

title: [API] employee-create: POST /api/employees with all valid fields returns 201 and persists employee
preconditions: Application is running with seeded employees.
POST /api/employees endpoint is reachable.
A unique email is generated using a timestamp suffix (e.g. test+<timestamp>@example.com) to avoid DUPLICATE_EMAIL conflicts on repeated runs.

steps.action: Send POST /api/employees with Content-Type: application/json and a complete valid JSON body containing all required fields.
steps.expectedResult: HTTP 201 Created is returned. Response Content-Type header is application/json.

steps.action: Assert the response body contains a top-level `_id` field that is a non-empty string.
steps.expectedResult: `_id` is present and non-empty, confirming the record was persisted with a database-generated identifier.

steps.action: Assert the response body field `firstName` equals the value sent in the request.
steps.expectedResult: `firstName` in response matches the submitted value exactly.

steps.action: Assert the response body field `lastName` equals the value sent in the request.
steps.expectedResult: `lastName` in response matches the submitted value exactly.

steps.action: Assert the response body field `email` equals the unique email sent in the request.
steps.expectedResult: `email` in response matches the submitted unique email exactly.

steps.action: Assert the response body fields `designation`, `department`, `employmentType`, `employmentStatus`, and `startDate` match the submitted values.
steps.expectedResult: All five fields are present in the response and equal the submitted values.

steps.action: Assert the response body contains an `address` object with `street`, `city`, and `country` matching the submitted values.
steps.expectedResult: `address.street` = "123 Test St", `address.city` = "Test City", `address.country` = "United States".

steps.action: Using the `_id` returned in step 1, send GET /api/employees/<_id> to confirm the record was persisted.
steps.expectedResult: HTTP 200 OK is returned. Response body `_id` matches the created employee's ID and `email` matches the unique email used in step 1.

steps.action: Send DELETE /api/employees/<_id> to clean up the test employee created in step 1.
steps.expectedResult: HTTP 200 or 204 is returned, confirming the test employee has been removed and will not pollute subsequent test runs.

expectedOutcome: POST /api/employees with a fully valid payload returns HTTP 201, a JSON body containing the persisted employee document with a database-generated `_id`, and all submitted fields reflected accurately. A subsequent GET confirms persistence. Cleanup DELETE removes the test record.

title: [API] employee-create: POST /api/employees with missing required fields returns 400 and descriptive error
preconditions: Application is running with seeded employees.
POST /api/employees endpoint is reachable.
No prior state setup is required; this test sends intentionally invalid payloads and does not persist any data.

steps.action: Send POST /api/employees with Content-Type: application/json and an empty JSON body `{}`.
steps.expectedResult: HTTP 400 Bad Request is returned. Response Content-Type is application/json.

steps.action: Assert the response body contains an error message or validation errors array indicating that required fields are missing.
steps.expectedResult: Response body includes a non-empty `message`, `error`, or `errors` field describing the missing required fields (e.g. firstName, lastName, email, etc.).

steps.action: Send POST /api/employees with Content-Type: application/json and a body that includes all required fields EXCEPT `email`.
steps.expectedResult: HTTP 400 Bad Request is returned. Response Content-Type is application/json.

steps.action: Assert the response body references `email` as the missing or invalid field.
steps.expectedResult: Response body error message or errors array explicitly mentions `email` as a required field that is absent or invalid.

steps.action: Send POST /api/employees with Content-Type: application/json and a body that includes all required fields but sets `email` to a non-email string (e.g. "not-an-email").
steps.expectedResult: HTTP 400 or 422 is returned. Response Content-Type is application/json.

steps.action: Assert the response body references `email` as the invalid field and does NOT return a 201 or include an `_id`.
steps.expectedResult: Response body error message mentions `email` format validation failure. No `_id` field is present in the response.

steps.action: Send POST /api/employees with Content-Type: application/json and a body that omits the `address` field entirely.
steps.expectedResult: HTTP 400 Bad Request is returned. Response Content-Type is application/json.

steps.action: Assert the response body references `address` as a missing or required field and does NOT include an `_id`.
steps.expectedResult: Response body error message or errors array mentions `address`. No `_id` is present, confirming no record was created.

expectedOutcome: POST /api/employees with missing or malformed required fields consistently returns HTTP 400 (or 422) with a JSON error body that identifies the offending fields. No employee record is created for any of the invalid payloads.

title: [UI] employee-create: Add employee form shows validation errors when submitted with missing required fields
preconditions: Application is running with seeded employees.

steps.action: Navigate to /employees in the browser.
steps.expectedResult: The Employees page loads and the employee list is visible. An 'Add Employee' button is present.

steps.action: Leave all form fields blank (do not enter any data).
steps.expectedResult: All form fields remain empty. No validation messages are shown yet.

steps.action: Click the Save (or Submit) button inside the drawer without filling in any fields.

steps.action: Observe the form for inline validation error messages on required fields.
steps.expectedResult: Validation error messages appear beneath or beside each required field (e.g. 'First name is required', 'Last name is required', 'Email is required'). The drawer remains open.

steps.expectedResult: An email format validation error is displayed (e.g. 'Please enter a valid email address'). The form does not submit.

steps.action: Click the Cancel or close (X) button on the drawer.

steps.expectedResult: No employee with the partial test data appears in the list. The total employee count is unchanged.

expectedOutcome: The Add Employee form enforces required-field and format validation on submit. No employee record is created when the form is incomplete or contains invalid data. The drawer remains open until the user explicitly cancels.

title: [API] employee-delete: DELETE /api/employees/:id successfully removes an existing employee
preconditions: Application is running with the employees API accessible at /api/employees

steps.expectedResult: Response status 200 with JSON body containing '_id' equal to <employeeId>

steps.action: Send DELETE /api/employees/<employeeId> with header 'Content-Type: application/json'
steps.expectedResult: Response status 204 with an empty response body

steps.expectedResult: Response status 200 with a JSON array that does not contain any object with '_id' equal to <employeeId>

expectedOutcome: DELETE /api/employees/:id returns HTTP 204 with no body, and subsequent GET requests for the same ID return 404, confirming the record is permanently removed from the data store

preconditions: Application is running with the employees API accessible at /api/employees

steps.action: Assert the response Content-Type header contains 'application/json'
steps.expectedResult: Content-Type header value includes 'application/json'

steps.action: Send POST /api/employees to create a dedicated test employee
steps.expectedResult: Response status 201 with JSON body containing '_id', 'firstName', and 'email'

steps.action: Extract and store the '_id' value from the POST response body as <newEmployeeId>
steps.expectedResult: '_id' is a non-empty string

steps.action: Send DELETE /api/employees/<newEmployeeId> to perform the first (valid) deletion

title: [UI] employee-pagination: Clicking next page loads the second page of employees
preconditions: Application is running with seeded employees.
At least 11 employees exist in the system so that a second page is available (assuming 10 per page default).
User is on the Employees page at /employees (page 1).

steps.action: Navigate to /employees in the browser.
steps.expectedResult: The Employees page loads successfully. The employee table is visible and populated with the first page of employees.

steps.action: Observe and record the names (or any unique identifiers) of all employees currently displayed in the table on page 1.
steps.expectedResult: A list of employees is visible in the EmployeeTable component. The page indicator or pagination control shows 'Page 1' or equivalent.
steps.testData: selector: [data-testid=employee-table-row] or equivalent row selector in EmployeeTable.tsx

steps.action: Locate the pagination controls at the bottom of the employee table and verify the 'Next' (or '>' / 'Page 2') button is enabled and visible.
steps.expectedResult: The next-page button is rendered and not disabled, indicating more pages exist.

steps.action: Click the 'Next' page button.
steps.expectedResult: The table re-renders. A loading indicator may briefly appear. The table then displays a new set of employees different from those on page 1.

steps.action: Observe the pagination control or page indicator after the table updates.
steps.expectedResult: The current page indicator now shows 'Page 2' or equivalent (e.g., '11–20 of N').

steps.action: Verify that none of the employee rows now displayed in the table match the employee rows recorded from page 1 in step 2.
steps.expectedResult: All visible employee rows are distinct from the page-1 employees, confirming the correct page-2 data has been fetched and rendered.

steps.action: Verify the 'Previous' (or '<') pagination button is now enabled.
steps.expectedResult: The previous-page button is rendered and clickable, confirming the UI correctly reflects that the user is no longer on the first page.

steps.action: Click the 'Previous' page button.
steps.expectedResult: The table re-renders and displays the same set of employees that were visible on page 1 in step 2, confirming bidirectional navigation works correctly.

expectedOutcome: Clicking the next-page control fetches and renders the correct second page of employees, distinct from page 1. The page indicator updates accordingly, and navigating back to page 1 restores the original employee set.

title: [UI] employee-pagination: Previous button is disabled on the first page and next button is disabled on the last page
preconditions: Application is running with seeded employees.

steps.testData: selector: [data-testid=pagination-next-btn] or last page number button

expectedOutcome: The 'Previous' pagination button is disabled on page 1 and the 'Next' pagination button is disabled on the last page. Clicking either disabled button produces no navigation or error. Boundary page states are correctly enforced in the UI.
